text: Trump hints at an end to military action in Iran, saying U.S. will leave in 2-3 weeks - NPR
fullText: Trump's remarks came in response to a question about rising gas prices. He said they would fall once the U.S. leaves Iran, claiming that would happen in "maybe two weeks, maybe three."

text: American journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad - The Washington Post
fullText: Iraq’s interior ministry said it had arrested one suspect, seized a car and was looking for accomplices.

text: Nike Sales Miss in Middle East Region Sparks Investor Concern - Bloomberg.com
fullText: Nike Inc. shares tumbled after the retailer gave a surprisingly gloomy outlook for the year ahead, complicating Chief Executive Officer Elliott Hill’s efforts to turn around the business.

text: Judge orders Penn to comply with subpoena for information on Jewish groups - politico.com
fullText: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission is looking to gather evidence and identify potential victims of alleged acts of antisemitism at the school.

text: The Dow surges more than 1,100 points as Wall Street soars to its best day since last spring - AP News
fullText: U.S. stocks surged to their best day since last spring as doubt swung back to hope on Wall Street about a possible end to the war with Iran. The S&P 500 leaped 2.9% Tuesday for its largest gain since May. Just a day before, worries about the war had sent the …

text: Possible Measles Exposure Reported At O’Hare Airport - Block Club Chicago
fullText: A person who contracted the highly contagious and potentially deadly disease traveled through O'Hare's Terminal 5 and two public places in the suburbs, public health officials said.

text: Contact lost with Russian An-26 transport aircraft over occupied Crimea - The Kyiv Independent
fullText: A Russian An-26 transport aircraft crashed in occupied Crimea late on March 31, Russian Telegram media channels reported, killing 29 military personnel on board.

text: Starlink satellite breaks apart into "tens of objects"; SpaceX confirms "anomaly" - Ars Technica
fullText: Satellite failure cause is unexplained after second "fragment creation event."

text: Reality TV star Joseph Duggar posts $600,000 bond in Florida child molestation case - AP News

text: Artemis II mission is about to fly humans to the Moon — here’s the science they’ll do - Nature
fullText: Set to lift off this week, the NASA flight will take astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

text: Trump’s top litigator faces uphill battle with birthright citizenship - CNN
fullText: Sauer has become Trump’s favorite litigator after securing historic victories on issues such as presidential immunity and limiting the power of judges to block his policies nationwide.

text: ‘Summer House’ Stars Amanda Batula and West Wilson Confirm Romance Rumors, Ciara Miller Unfollows Both in Fallout - The Hollywood Reporter
fullText: Speculation of their relationship has been brewing to much backlash in the Bravosphere over the past few weeks, specifically because Ciara Miller, Batula's dear friend, previously dated Wilson.

text: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie review: It's-a mediocre - Roger Ebert
fullText: It's cute, and breezy, and rock-stupid, and will probably make a billion dollars again.

text: April’s full pink moon may coincide with a historic launch attempt - cnn.com
fullText: Named after spring blooms, April’s full pink moon rises Wednesday during the first night of the April launch window for the Artemis II human moon mission.

text: Supreme Court rules against ban on ‘conversion therapy’ for LGBTQ+ minors - The Washington Post
fullText: The major ruling casts doubt on similar prohibitions in 30 states. It is the latest decision by the high court rolling back protections for LGBTQ+ people.

text: Israel mandates death penalty for West Bank Palestinians who kill in terrorist acts - The Washington Post
fullText: Opposition lawmakers, rights advocates and some foreign governments condemned the law as discriminatory. Israelis in the territory are tried in different courts.
